root_cause: Sailly voice bot is rate-limiting WebSocket connections (HTTP 429) during the smoke test phase, likely due to burst request handling or missing connection pooling/backoff logic.

fix_actions.description: Review and increase WebSocket rate limit thresholds in Sailly config (check rate_limit_per_minute, burst_size, and time_window settings). If using a reverse proxy or API gateway, adjust its rate limiting rules.
Add exponential backoff with jitter to the test harness: implement 1s initial delay, 2x multiplier, max 10s between WebSocket reconnection attempts across test cases.
Implement connection pooling or queuing in the test phase_runner: limit concurrent WebSocket connections to ≤2 during smoke tests, serialize test execution rather than parallel burst.

Correlate HTTP 429 rejection timestamps with Sailly backend logs (check for rate limiter middleware logs, connection handler queue depth, and Deepgram/Gemini API quota usage). Verify no cascading failures from downstream STT/TTS services.
If rate limit config is already relaxed, perform a clean restart of sailly-browser-demo service on port 8080 with 30s warm-up grace period before resuming tests.
